goal: Extend UNIVERSAL_NOISE filtering to sprawl + slackers' affix-pattern detection. Measure dogfood deltas on the v1.2.0 snapshot. Decide based on observed impact whether to keep the filter, narrow it, or revert. Capture findings in docs/observations/composition/04.md regardless of outcome.

summary.title: Extend UNIVERSAL_NOISE to sprawl + slackers' affix detection — null impact on slop snapshot
summary.what: Extended _build_affix_patterns() to accept an exclude= argument (defaulting to empty frozenset) and threaded UNIVERSAL_NOISE through from sprawl.py and slackers.py callers. The filter strips Newman 14 + identifier-glue tokens from each Lexeme.lower before pairwise token-edit-distance computation. Captured the empirical result in docs/observations/composition/04.md: dogfood numbers identical (40 violations / 38 advisories / 52 waived; same per-rule breakdown) — the Newman 14 + glue do not appear as discriminative variant-position alphabet members in slop's own snapshot. 402/402 tests green.
summary.why: Closes the open question from the previous intent (int_367818ce): the lexicon-port commit applied UNIVERSAL_NOISE to imposters' modal-token analysis only and deliberately left affix-pattern detection unfiltered for separate measurement. This intent runs that measurement. The result is a clean null on this corpus, but the filter is kept as the default anyway — it is literature-grounded (Newman 2017 measured these tokens across 50 OSS systems), the cost is one frozenset lookup per token, and the alternative ('include Newman 14 in alphabet detection') is harder to defend than the filter's presence.
summary.user_goal: Extend UNIVERSAL_NOISE filtering to sprawl + slackers' affix-pattern detection. Measure dogfood deltas on the v1.2.0 snapshot. Decide based on observed impact whether to keep the filter, narrow it, or revert. Capture findings in docs/observations/composition/04.md regardless of outcome.

decisions.chose: Kept the filter as the default after measuring zero impact on slop's snapshot
Filter applied at the _build_affix_patterns level, not pushed up into Lexicon
Both sprawl and slackers updated in the same commit
Wrote observation 04 even though the result is a null

architectural_claims: _build_affix_patterns is a Lexicon-adjacent helper (consumes Lexemes, lives in sprawl.py) rather than a Lexicon method, because its algorithm is pairwise edit-distance + alphabet clustering, structurally distinct from frequency/modal queries.
UNIVERSAL_NOISE is the literature-grounded floor for affix detection; richer per-rule ignore lists belong in extra_ignored= configuration where the trade-off is explicit per call site.
behavioral_changes: _build_affix_patterns now strips exclude= tokens from Lexeme.lower before pairwise edit-distance. Both consumers (sprawl, slackers) pass UNIVERSAL_NOISE.
Snapshot dogfood numbers unchanged from baseline: 40 / 38 / 52 across 13 firing rules. Documented in docs/observations/composition/04.md.
migration_notes: Future calibration on multi-language / less-curated corpora should use this same filter set; if a different ignore vocabulary is required for a specific corpus, configure it via extra_ignored= per rule rather than expanding UNIVERSAL_NOISE itself.
